zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathilde Ire de Bourbon於1165年6月12日出生在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯阿利也布爾波-利阿哈波奧爾特時，她的父親Archambaud VIII de BOURBON24歲，母親Alix de Bourgogne20歲。她於1196年與Guy II de Dampierre結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1228年6月18日在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易豐特福勞爾吉利阿巴易享年63歲，埋葬在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易利阿巴易。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourbon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from a village in Allier, the site of the (now ruined) castle of Bourbon, or from another place called (Le) Bourbon, mainly in the southern part of France. The placename is derived from a Celtic and pre-Celtic element borb- denoting a hot spring.

History: The progenitor of a large part of the American bearers of the surname Bourbon was Jean-Pierre Bourbon from Salins-les-Bains in Jura, France, who settled in MO in the middle of the 19th century. However, many bearers of the surname claim a connection with the house of Bourbon, which provided generations of monarchs of France and Spain and other European royalty. It takes its name from the castle of Bourbon in Allier, which was held by Adhémar, a 9th-century noble. His descendant Beatrice, heiress of Bourbon, married Robert of Clermont, 6th son of King Louis IX of France, in 1272, and these two are considered founders of the royal house. Robert's son Louis was created Duke of Bourbon in 1327. By the 16th century they had added much of southern France, the dukedom of Vendôme, and the kingdom of Navarre to their fiefs, and in 1589 a Bourbon succeeded to the throne of France as Henry IV. His grandson, Louis XIV, called ‘the Sun King’, reigned 1643–1715, presiding over a golden age of French literature and art and attempting to establish French supremacy in Europe. The Bourbons ruled France until the Revolution in 1793 and again 1815–48. The present claimants to the French throne are descended from Louis XIV's brother, Philippe, Duke of Orléans. The Bourbons acquired the Spanish throne in 1700, when a grandson of Louis XIV succeeded as Philip V; they ruled until 1931 and were restored, in the person of King Juan Carlos, in 1975. The Bourbon kings of the Two Sicilies (i.e. Sicily and Naples), 1759–1861, and Bourbon dukes of Parma, 1748–1860, were descended from the Spanish branch of the family.

variants.name: Borbon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ana, Jesus, Marta, Miguel Angel, Tomas, Ademar, Alfredo, Ana Maria, Bernabe, Berta, Carlos.

nameMeanings.name: Mathilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: Ire
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Mathilde Ire de Bourbon was born on 12 June 1165, in Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, her father, Archambaud VIII de BOURBON, was 24 and her mother, Alix de Bourgogne, was 20. She married Guy II de Dampierre in 1196. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 18 June 1228, in Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France, at the age of 63, and was buried in L'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathilde Ire de Bourbon est née le 12 juin 1165, à Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, son père, Archambaud VIII de BOURBON, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Alix de Bourgogne, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Guy II de Dampierre en 1196. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée le 18 juin 1228, à Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à L'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mathilde Ire de Bourbon am 12. Juni 1165 in Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Archambaud VIII de BOURBON 24 und ihre Mutter Alix de Bourgogne 20. Sie hat 1196 Guy II de Dampierre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 18. Juni 1228 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich und wurde in L'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde Ire de Bourbon è nata il il 12 giugno 1165 nel Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, sua padre, Archambaud VIII de BOURBON, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Alix de Bourgogne, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Guy II de Dampierre nel 1196. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 18 giugno 1228, nel Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolta a L'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mathilde Ire de Bourbonが1165年6月12日にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスアリールボウルボン-ル'アルチャンバウルトで生まれた時，父Archambaud VIII de BOURBONは24歳，母Alix de Bourgogneは20歳であった。1196年にGuy II de Dampierreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1228年6月18日にフランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーメインエ-エト-ロイアーフォンテvラウルト-ル'アッバイで63歳で死去し，フランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーメインエ-エト-ロイアール'アッバイにて埋葬された。

ko.Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pa: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 폰테브라트-'압베이
ko.L'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loi: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 '압베이
ko.Fontevraud-L'Abbaye,49140,Maine-et-Loire: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 폰테브라트-'압베이
ko.lifeSummary: Mathilde Ire de Bourbon(이)가 1165년 6월 12일 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 앨리어 부르보-'아르챔바트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Archambaud VIII de BOURBON(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alix de Bourgogne(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1196년 Guy II de Dampierre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1228년 6월 18일 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 폰테브라트-'압베이에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 '압베이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde Ire de Bourbon nasceu em 12 de junho de 1165, em Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França, seu pai, Archambaud VIII de BOURBON, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Alix de Bourgogne, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Guy II de Dampierre em 1196. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 18 de junho de 1228, em Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França, com 63 anos, e foi sepultada em L'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França.

ru.Fontevraud-L'Abbaye,49140,Maine-et-Loire: фонтевраулт-л'аббеи, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathilde Ire de Bourbon родилась 12 июня 1165 г. в местности бурбон-л'арчамбаулт, аллиир, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция, её отцу по имени Archambaud VIII de BOURBON было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Alix de Bourgogne, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guy II de Dampierre в 1196 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла 18 июня 1228 г. в местности фонтевраулт-л'аббеи, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности л'аббеи, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mathilde Ire de Bourbon nació el 12 de junio de 1165, en Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, su papá, Archambaud VIII de BOURBON, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Alix de Bourgogne, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Guy II de Dampierre en 1196. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 18 de junio de 1228, en Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultada en L'Abbaye, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia.
